content.full_text:
Re
Fern Por-rrrcAt- PnecrrcEs C oMMrssroN
-128 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacrarnento. CA 9-581"1-2329
(916) -122-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
April23,2009
James G. Gubetta
202 Woodridge Court
Weed, CA96094
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09-087
Dear Mr. Gubetta:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r In addition, this letter is based on the
facts presented. The Fair Political Practices Commission ("the Commission") does not
act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance. (ln re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.)
Also, please note that our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We
therefore offer no opinion on the application, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws
such as common law conflict of interest or Government Code Section 1090.
QrrEsTroN
Considering the fact that there are three vacant positions on your five member
board and the improbability that two of the three vacancies will be filled in the
foreseeable future, does the legally required participation exception allow you to
participate in a decision regarding a permit for a project within 500 feet of your home if
your participation is needed to constitute a quorum?
CONCLUSION
Absent additional facts, we do not find that multiple vacancies resulting from a
political failure of the appointing authority to fill the vacancies are sufficient to warrant
the use of the legally required participation exception.
I The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections I 8 I l0 through I 8997 of Title 2 of the Calitbrnia
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. .{-09-087
Page No. 2
You are the Chairman for the Siskiyou County Air Pollution Control Board (the
"Board")' The Board has a total of five poiitionr. oi ttre five positions, three positions
are currently vacant. While there is one appointment pending,lt is unlikely that two of
the vacant positions will be filled in the foriseeabte fuiure.
Additionally, the Board will soon consider a permit for a project located within
500 feet (less than 300 feet to be more exact) of property where you reside. erru*ing
you are disqualified from participating in the permif aecision under the Act,s conflict-of-
interest provisions, you have asked foi guidance on the application of the ..legaily
required participation" exception.
FACTS
AIIALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-iriterest provisions ensure that public officials will .,perform
their duties in an impartial manner, frie from bias caused by their own financial interests
or the financial interests of persons wlo have supported them.,, (Section gl00l(b).)
Section 87100 prohibits any public official frorn-making, participating in making, or
otherwise using his or her official position to influenc. i'iou"**ental decision in which
the official has a financial interest.
The commissio-ntras adopted an eighrstep standard analysis for deciding whether
an official has a disqualifying conflict of interest.'(Regulation rgzoo(u).) vour inquiry
only concems the last.step. More specifically, y9q in[uiry only concems the question of
whether you may participate in a decision, in which you ui" othlrwise disqualified from
participating, under the "legally required participation" exception.
The Act recognizes that there may be instances where a governrnental body
cannot function without the participationof a public official *rri rr"r " ""rni"i"r
interest' The Act contains a nalTow exception to the conflict-of-interest rules, codified at
Section 87101, allowing an official otherwise disqualified from a gou"*lriai decision
to participate in the decision if the official's participation is "legally required.,, However,
this exception is narrowly construed and appiies oniy where there is no.,alternative
source of decision consistent_with the purposes and ierms of the statute authorizing the
decision." (Regulation lg7Og(a)(c): Ii rirotias (1999) l3 Fppc ops.5.)
In determining whether the "legally.required participation" exception applies in
the context of a vacancy that could ue iitteo uy appoint..ri, we have pi"uiouriy
determined that the exceptiondid not apply unr"s it was lelaly impossible ro ritt ,t.
vacant position. (Hunter Advice Letter, No. 4-99-088.) However, in an opinion in 1999,
the Commission subsequently determined that the "legally required participation,,
exception may still apply in circumstances in which ii*as possible io fill tihe vacancy if


File No. 4-09-087
Page No. 3
other factors existed that warranted the use of the exception, even when the vacancy was
the result of the political failure of the appointing authority to fill the vacancy.
In reaching this conclusion, the Commission provided the following explanation:
"In determining whether the Rule applied in the context of a
vacancy, the Commission looked to a numbir of factors, which included:
the nature of the decision; whether there was an alternative method of
decisionmaking consistent with the pulpose and functions of the parti.cular
agency, whether the agency cou-ld have changed the quorum r"quir"-ert.,
or appointed altemative or interim members *ho couid vote; whether the
decisiqn had to be made within a specified time period; and the
importance of the agency moving forward.', (In're Tobias, supra;also see
Danforth Advice Letter, No. A_05_23g.)
Notwithstanding the fact that the legal possibility or impossibility to fill a vacant
position does not conclusively determine whetler the lelally required purri"ipu,io"
exception applies, the only information you have provid-ed in your request ror aarice is
that three of five positions on the Board are ,u"unt, and that iiis untGty that two of the
vacancies will be filled in the foreseeable future. However, to determine whether the
legally required participation exception applies to your specific circumstances, all factors
including, but not limited to, the urgency oi the aelision, the Board,s or controlling
authority's ability to modify quorum requirements or appoint interim member, and the
possibility of an alternative method of decisionmaking such as leaving the decision to the
Siskiyou county Board of Supervisors must be consiJered.
Accordingly, we do not find the multiple vacancies resulting from a political
failure gf the appointing authority to fill the vacancies are sufficienlto *u11*t the use of
the legally required participation exception absent additional information pr*uiring,o
the factors identified in the In re Tobiis opinion.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contacr me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division
BGLjgl
